html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Nineteen — Part One

The Book of Yehezkel

YEHEZKEL

The Book of Ezekiel — 4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek Primary

Chapters 1 through 11

The Merkavah Vision — The Call — The Temple Abominations — The Kavod Departs — A Little Sanctuary

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 and Cave 11 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 11 — THE MERKAVAH VISION AND THE DEPARTURE OF THE KAVOD

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 1 The Vision of the Chariot — The Four Living Creatures — The Wheels Within Wheels — The Likeness of the Firmament — The Appearance of the Fire

1 In the thirtieth year, in the fourth month, on the fifth day of the month, as I was among the exiles by the Kevar river, the heavens were opened, and I saw visions of YHWH.

2 On the fifth day of the month — it was the fifth year of the exile of King Yekonyahu —

3 The word of YHWH came to Yehezkel the priest, the son of Buzi, in the land of the Chaldeans by the Kevar river, and the hand of YHWH was upon him there.

4 As I looked, behold, a stormy wind came out of the north, and a great cloud, with brightness around it, and fire flashing forth continually, and in the midst of the fire, as it were gleaming metal.

5 And from the midst of it came the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: they had a human likeness,

6 But each had four faces, and each of them had four wings.

7 Their legs were straight, and the soles of their feet were like the sole of a calf's foot. And they sparkled like burnished bronze.

8 Under their wings on their four sides they had human hands. And the four had their faces and their wings thus:

9 Their wings touched one another. Each one of them went straight forward, without turning as they went.

10 As for the likeness of their faces: each had a human face in front, each of the four had the face of a lion on the right side, each of the four had the face of an ox on the left side, and each of the four had the face of an eagle at the back.

11 Such were their faces. And their wings were spread out above. Each creature had two wings, each of which touched the wing of another, while two covered their bodies.

12 And each went straight forward. Wherever the spirit would go, they went, without turning as they went.

13 As for the likeness of the living creatures, their appearance was like burning coals of fire, like the appearance of torches moving to and fro among the living creatures. And the fire was bright, and out of the fire went lightning.

14 And the living creatures darted to and fro, like the appearance of a flash of lightning.

15 Now as I looked at the living creatures, I saw a wheel on the earth beside the living creatures, one for each of the four of them.

16 As for the appearance of the wheels and their construction: their appearance was like the gleaming of beryl. And the four had the same likeness, their appearance and construction being as it were a wheel within a wheel.

17 When they went, they went in any of their four directions without turning as they went.

18 And their rims were tall and awesome, and the rims of all four were full of eyes all around.

19 And when the living creatures went, the wheels went beside them. And when the living creatures rose from the earth, the wheels rose.

20 Wherever the spirit wanted to go, they went, and the wheels rose along with them, for the spirit of the living creatures was in the wheels.

21 When those went, these went. And when those stood, these stood. And when those rose from the earth, the wheels rose along with them, for the spirit of the living creatures was in the wheels.

22 Over the heads of the living creatures there was the likeness of a firmament, shining like awe-inspiring crystal, spread out above their heads.

23 And under the firmament their wings were stretched out straight, one toward another. And each creature had two wings covering its body.

24 And when they went, I heard the sound of their wings like the sound of many waters, like the sound of the Almighty, a sound of tumult like the sound of an army. When they stood still, they let down their wings.

25 And there came a voice from above the firmament over their heads. When they stood still, they let down their wings.

26 And above the firmament over their heads there was the likeness of a throne, in appearance like sapphire. And seated above the likeness of a throne was a likeness with a human appearance.

27 And upward from what had the appearance of his waist I saw as it were gleaming metal, like the appearance of fire enclosed all around. And downward from what had the appearance of his waist I saw as it were the appearance of fire, and there was brightness around him.

28 Like the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud on the day of rain, so was the appearance of the brightness all around. Such was the appearance of the likeness of the kavod of YHWH. And when I saw it, I fell on my face, and I heard the voice of one speaking.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QEzeka and 4QEzekb preserve portions of Yehezkel. The Merkavah vision of Chapter 1 is the most complex prophetic vision in the entire Hebrew canon. The four living creatures — each with four faces — the wheels within wheels full of eyes, the crystal firmament, the sapphire throne: these are not literary embellishment but covenant ontological disclosure. YHWH's covenant throne-chariot comes to Babylon to commission a prophet among the exiles.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 28 — like the appearance of the bow in the cloud on the day of rain — the covenant rainbow from Bereshit 9 is the closest known visual comparison to YHWH's kavod. The covenant memory of the Noahic bow anchors the most extreme visionary experience in the covenant tradition. YHWH's appearance to the exiles in Babylon is a covenant appearance, not an abandonment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. The Merkavah vision has been the central text of Jewish mystical tradition for millennia. Verse 3 — by the Kevar river in the land of the Chaldeans. YHWH's kavod appears not in Yerushalayim but in Babylon. The covenant presence is not geographically confined to the covenant land. This is the foundational covenant statement of the entire book.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — in the thirtieth year. The specific covenant chronological marker that has generated centuries of interpretive debate: thirtieth year of what? The most internally consistent reading is the thirtieth year of Yehezkel's own life — making this his covenant call vision at the age when priests entered full service according to Bemidbar 4:3. The covenant priest who cannot serve in the destroyed temple receives the covenant Merkavah vision instead.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 2 The Call of Yehezkel — Son of Man, Stand on Your Feet — They Are a Rebellious House — The Scroll Given to Him

1 And he said to me, Son of man, stand on your feet, and I will speak with you.

2 And as he spoke to me, the Spirit entered into me and set me on my feet, and I heard him speaking to me.

3 And he said to me, Son of man, I send you to the people of Yisra'EL, to nations of rebels, who have rebelled against me. They and their fathers have transgressed against me to this very day.

4 The descendants also are impudent and stubborn: I send you to them, and you shall say to them, Thus says the Creator YHWH.

5 And whether they hear or refuse to hear — for they are a rebellious house — they will know that a prophet has been among them.

6 And you, son of man, be not afraid of them, nor be afraid of their words, though briers and thorns are with you and you sit on scorpions. Be not afraid of their words, nor be dismayed at their looks, for they are a rebellious house.

7 And you shall speak my words to them, whether they hear or refuse to hear, for they are a rebellious house.

8 But you, son of man, hear what I say to you. Be not rebellious like that rebellious house. Open your mouth and eat what I give you.

9 And when I looked, behold, a hand was stretched out to me, and behold, a scroll of a book was in it.

10 And he spread it before me. And it had writing on the front and on the back, and there were written on it words of lamentation and mourning and woe.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The address Ben Adam — Son of Man — used throughout Yehezkel is the covenant reminder of Yehezkel's human station before the overwhelming covenant presence of Chapter 1. He is called to stand — the Spirit enables what the vision has prostrated. The covenant commission to a rebellious house begins with the covenant acknowledgment that the prophet cannot stand in this presence without divine enablement.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 5 — whether they hear or refuse to hear, they will know that a prophet has been among them — is the covenant criterion of prophetic success: not response but presence. Yehezkel is not commissioned to produce a result but to deliver a covenant word. The covenant word's reception is not the prophet's covenant responsibility.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 10 — on the scroll were written words of lamentation and mourning and woe, written on front and back. The covenant scroll given to Yehezkel is the covenant content of his entire ministry written in advance: the covenant message is predetermined by YHWH, not generated by the prophet's observation of current events.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — briers and thorns are with you and you sit on scorpions. The covenant environmental metaphor for the hostile covenant community: the prophet's context is not merely indifference but active hostile resistance. The covenant command is to speak anyway. The covenant courage required is not the absence of awareness of danger but the decision to speak despite full awareness of it.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 3 Eat This Scroll — Sweet as Honey — I Send You to the House of Yisra'EL — The Watchman — The Hand of YHWH Upon Him

1 And he said to me, Son of man, eat whatever you find here. Eat this scroll, and go, speak to the house of Yisra'EL.

2 So I opened my mouth, and he gave me this scroll to eat.

3 And he said to me, Son of man, feed your belly with this scroll that I give you and fill your stomach with it. Then I ate it, and it was in my mouth as sweet as honey.

4 And he said to me, Son of man, go to the house of Yisra'EL and speak with my words to them.

5 For you are not sent to a people of foreign speech and a hard language, but to the house of Yisra'EL —

6 Not to many peoples of foreign speech and a hard language, whose words you cannot understand. Surely, if I sent you to such, they would listen to you.

7 But the house of Yisra'EL will not be willing to listen to you, for they are not willing to listen to me: because all the house of Yisra'EL have a hard forehead and a stubborn heart.

8 Behold, I have made your face as hard as their faces, and your forehead as hard as their foreheads.

9 Like emery harder than flint have I made your forehead. Fear them not, nor be dismayed at their looks, for they are a rebellious house.

10 Moreover, he said to me, Son of man, all my words that I shall speak to you receive in your heart, and hear with your ears.

11 And go to the exiles, to your people, and speak to them and say to them, Thus says the Creator YHWH, whether they hear or refuse to hear.

12 Then the Spirit lifted me up, and I heard behind me the voice of a great earthquake: Blessed be the kavod of YHWH from his place.

13 It was the sound of the wings of the living creatures as they touched one another, and the sound of the wheels beside them, and the sound of a great earthquake.

14 The Spirit lifted me up and took me away, and I went in bitterness in the heat of my spirit, the hand of YHWH being strong upon me.

15 And I came to the exiles at Tel-Aviv, who were living by the Kevar river, and I sat there stunned among them seven days.

16 And at the end of seven days, the word of YHWH came to me:

17 Son of man, I have made you a watchman for the house of Yisra'EL. Whenever you hear a word from my mouth, you shall give them warning from me.

18 If I say to the wicked, You shall surely die, and you give him no warning, nor speak to warn the wicked from his wicked way, in order to save his life, that wicked person shall die for his iniquity, but his blood I will require at your hand.

19 But if you warn the wicked, and he does not turn from his wickedness, or from his wicked way, he shall die for his iniquity, but you will have delivered your soul.

20 Again, if a righteous person turns from his righteousness and commits injustice, and I lay a stumbling block before him, he shall die. Because you have not warned him, he shall die for his sin, and his righteous deeds that he has done shall not be remembered, but his blood I will require at your hand.

21 But if you warn the righteous person not to sin, and he does not sin, he shall surely live, because he took warning, and you will have delivered your soul.

22 And the hand of YHWH was upon me there. And he said to me, Arise, go out into the valley, and there I will speak with you.

23 So I arose and went out into the valley, and behold, the kavod of YHWH stood there, like the kavod that I had seen by the Kevar river, and I fell on my face.

24 But the Spirit entered into me and set me on my feet, and he spoke with me and said to me, Go, shut yourself within your house.

25 And you, son of man, behold, cords will be placed upon you, and you shall be bound with them, so that you cannot go out among the people.

26 And I will make your tongue cling to the roof of your mouth, so that you shall be mute and unable to reprove them, for they are a rebellious house.

27 But when I speak with you, I will open your mouth, and you shall say to them, Thus says the Creator YHWH. He who will hear, let him hear. And he who will refuse to hear, let him refuse, for they are a rebellious house.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 3 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The sweet scroll — verse 3 — consumed and tasted as honey is the covenant parallel to Yirmeyahu's consuming of the covenant word — Yirmeyahu 15:16. The covenant word that pronounces judgment tastes sweet to the covenant prophet because it is YHWH's word — regardless of its content. The covenant watchman commission of verses 17-21 is the most precise individual covenant responsibility statement in the prophetic corpus.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 15 — I came to the exiles at Tel-Aviv and sat there stunned among them seven days. The covenant prophet does not immediately speak: he sits in silence among the exiles for seven days. The covenant word is not a reflex — it requires the covenant immersion in the situation of those to whom it is sent.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verses 18-21 — the watchman covenant: if the wicked die unwarned, their blood is on the watchman; if warned and they refuse, their blood is on themselves. The covenant transfer of responsibility through faithful warning is the covenant jurisprudence of prophetic ministry. The prophet's covenant obligation is warning, not conversion.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — I went in bitterness in the heat of my spirit. Yehezkel goes to the exiles not with covenant enthusiasm but with covenant anguish. The hand of YHWH is strong upon him — the covenant compulsion is not welcome but unavoidable. The covenant prophet's internal state is one of grief, not triumph. This is the covenant authenticity of prophetic ministry: the covenant word does not produce covenant joy in the prophet who must deliver judgment to his own people in exile.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 4 The Siege of Yerushalayim Enacted — The Brick and the Siege Works — Lying on His Side — Bread Baked With Dung

1 And you, son of man, take a brick and lay it before you, and engrave on it a city, even Yerushalayim.

2 And put the siege works against it, and build a siege wall against it, and cast up a mound against it. Set camps also against it, and plant battering rams against it all around.

3 And take an iron griddle, and place it as an iron wall between you and the city. And set your face toward it, and let it be in a state of siege, and press the siege against it. This is a sign for the house of Yisra'EL.

4 Then lie on your left side, and place the punishment of the house of Yisra'EL upon it. For the number of the days that you lie on it, you shall bear their punishment.

5 For I assign to you a number of days, 390 days, equal to the number of the years of their punishment. So long shall you bear the punishment of the house of Yisra'EL.

6 And when you have completed these, you shall lie down a second time, but on your right side, and bear the punishment of the house of Yehudah. Forty days I assign you, a day for each year.

7 And you shall set your face toward the siege of Yerushalayim, with your arm bared, and you shall prophesy against it.

8 And behold, I will place cords upon you, so that you cannot turn from one side to the other, till you have completed the days of your siege.

9 And you, take wheat and barley, beans and lentils, millet and emmer, and put them into a single vessel and make your bread from them. During the number of days that you lie on your side, 390 days, you shall eat it.

10 And your food that you eat shall be by weight, twenty shekels a day. From day to day you shall eat it.

11 And water you shall drink by measure, the sixth part of a hin. From day to day you shall drink.

12 And you shall eat it as a barley cake, baking it in their sight on human dung.

13 And YHWH said, Thus shall the people of Yisra'EL eat their bread unclean, among the nations where I will drive them.

14 Then I said, Ah, Creator YHWH. Behold, I have never defiled myself. From my youth up till now I have never eaten what died of itself or was torn by beasts, nor has tainted meat come into my mouth.

15 Then he said to me, See, I assign to you cow's dung instead of human dung, on which you may prepare your bread.

16 Moreover, he said to me, Son of man, behold, I will break the supply of bread in Yerushalayim. They shall eat bread by weight and with anxiety, and they shall drink water by measure and in dismay.

17 I will do this that they may lack bread and water, and look at one another in dismay, and rot away because of their punishment.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 4 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The sign-acts of Chapter 4 are the covenant enactment theater of Yehezkel's ministry: he does not merely speak covenant judgment — he performs it with his body. The 390 days and 40 days — total 430 — echo the covenant sojourn number of the Mitzrayim captivity in Shemot 12:40. The covenant numbers connect the Babylonian exile to the Mitzrayim exile.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 14 — I have never defiled myself — I have never eaten what died of itself or was torn by beasts. Yehezkel's covenant protest at the bread-baked-on-dung command is the covenant priest maintaining covenant purity standards even in the covenant sign-act context. YHWH accommodates the covenant priestly concern — verse 15 — by substituting cow dung. The covenant law is not suspended for prophetic theater.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 4. The bread of verse 9 — wheat, barley, beans, lentils, millet, emmer in one vessel — is the famine bread of siege: every available grain combined into a single survival loaf. The covenant sign-act physically enacts the food scarcity of the coming siege. Yehezkel's body becomes the covenant document.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5-6 — 390 days for the house of Yisra'EL, 40 days for the house of Yehudah. The covenant arithmetic of punishment: 390 years of accumulated covenant sin for the northern kingdom, 40 years for the southern. The covenant precision of divine accounting: YHWH does not punish arbitrarily but according to a measured covenant ledger. The exile is not chaotic divine anger — it is calibrated covenant discipline.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 5 The Razor and the Hair — A Third Burned, a Third Struck, a Third Scattered — Because of Your Abominations

1 And you, son of man, take a sharp sword. Use it as a barber's razor and pass it over your head and your beard. Then take balances for weighing and divide the hair.

2 A third part you shall burn in the fire in the midst of the city, when the days of the siege are completed. And a third part you shall take and strike with the sword all around the city. And a third part you shall scatter to the wind, and I will unsheathe the sword after them.

3 And you shall take from these a small number and bind them in the skirts of your robe.

4 And of these again you shall take some and cast them into the midst of the fire and burn them in the fire. From there a fire will come out into all the house of Yisra'EL.

5 Thus says the Creator YHWH: This is Yerushalayim. I have set her in the center of the nations, with countries all around her.

6 And she has rebelled against my rules by doing wickedness more than the nations, and against my statutes more than the countries all around her. For they have rejected my rules and have not walked in my statutes.

7 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Because you are more turbulent than the nations that are all around you, and have not walked in my statutes or obeyed my rules, and have not even acted according to the rules of the nations that are all around you,

8 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Behold, I, even I, am against you. And I will execute judgments in your midst in the sight of the nations.

9 And because of all your abominations I will do with you what I have never yet done, and the like of which I will never do again.

10 Therefore fathers shall eat their sons in your midst, and sons shall eat their fathers. And I will execute judgments on you, and any of you who survive I will scatter to all the winds.

11 Therefore, as I live, declares the Creator YHWH, surely, because you have defiled my sanctuary with all your detestable things and with all your abominations, therefore I will withdraw. My eye will not spare, and I will have no pity.

12 A third part of you shall die of pestilence and be consumed with famine in your midst. A third part shall fall by the sword all around you. And a third part I will scatter to all the winds and will unsheathe the sword after them.

13 Thus shall my anger spend itself, and I will vent my fury upon them and satisfy myself. And they shall know that I am YHWH — that I have spoken in my jealousy — when I spend my fury upon them.

14 Moreover, I will make you a desolation and an object of reproach among the nations all around you and in the sight of all who pass by.

15 You shall be a reproach and a taunt, a warning and a horror, to the nations all around you, when I execute judgments on you in anger and fury, and with furious rebukes — I am YHWH who has spoken —

16 When I send against you the deadly arrows of famine, arrows for destruction, which I will send to destroy you, and when I bring more and more famine upon you and break your supply of bread.

17 I will send famine and wild beasts against you, and they will rob you of your children. Pestilence and blood shall pass through you, and I will bring the sword upon you. I am YHWH who has spoken.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 5 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The razor sign-act — the prophet shaves head and beard and divides the hair into thirds — is the covenant visual of the fate of Yerushalayim's population: a third destroyed in the city by pestilence and famine, a third by the sword, a third scattered. Verse 5 — I have set her in the center of the nations — is the covenant geographic claim: Yerushalayim's covenant responsibility is proportional to its covenant centrality.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 9 — because of all your abominations I will do with you what I have never yet done, and the like of which I will never do again — is the covenant declaration of unique judgment: the fall of Yerushalayim is not simply one more conquest but the singular covenant judgment that has no precedent and no repetition in its specific character.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 5. Verse 6 — she has rebelled against my rules by doing wickedness more than the nations. The covenant indictment of Yerushalayim is that she has exceeded the covenant violations of the surrounding nations — even those nations without the covenant. The covenant standard for those within the covenant is categorically higher than the standard for those outside it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — thus shall my anger spend itself, and I will satisfy myself. The covenant language of YHWH's wrath reaching its designated end: the judgment is not perpetual. It has a covenant completion point at which YHWH's anger is exhausted. The covenant severity of the judgment is proportional to the covenant centrality of the city — but it is bounded. YHWH's covenant fury is not infinite — it is commensurate with the covenant violation and has a terminus.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 6 Prophecy Against the Mountains of Yisra'EL — The High Places Destroyed — A Remnant Shall Escape — You Shall Know That I Am YHWH

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 Son of man, set your face toward the mountains of Yisra'EL, and prophesy against them,

3 And say, You mountains of Yisra'EL, hear the word of the Creator YHWH. Thus says the Creator YHWH to the mountains and the hills, to the ravines and the valleys: Behold, I, even I, will bring a sword upon you, and I will destroy your high places.

4 Your altars shall become desolate, and your incense altars shall be broken, and I will cast down your slain before your idols.

5 And I will lay the dead bodies of the people of Yisra'EL before their idols, and I will scatter your bones around your altars.

6 Wherever you dwell, the cities shall be waste and the high places ruined, so that your altars will be waste and ruined, your idols broken and destroyed, your incense altars cut down, and your works wiped out.

7 And the slain shall fall in your midst, and you shall know that I am YHWH.

8 Yet I will leave some of you alive. When you have among the nations some who escape the sword, and when you are scattered through the countries,

9 Then those of you who escape will remember me among the nations where they are carried captive, how I have been broken over their whoring heart that has departed from me and over their eyes that go whoring after their idols. And they will be loathsome in their own sight for the evils that they have committed, for all their abominations.

10 And they shall know that I am YHWH. I have not said in vain that I would do this evil to them.

11 Thus says the Creator YHWH: Clap your hands and stamp your foot and say, Alas, because of all the evil abominations of the house of Yisra'EL, for they shall fall by sword, by famine, and by pestilence.

12 He who is far off shall die of pestilence, and he who is near shall fall by the sword, and he who is left and is preserved shall die of famine. Thus I will spend my fury upon them.

13 And you shall know that I am YHWH, when their slain lie among their idols around their altars, on every high hill, on all the mountaintops, under every green tree, and under every leafy oak, wherever they offered pleasing aroma to all their idols.

14 And I will stretch out my hand against them and make the land desolate and waste, in all their dwelling places, from the wilderness to Rivlah. Then they will know that I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 6 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The oracle against the mountains of Yisra'EL is the covenant address to the landscape of the covenant land itself: the high places — bamot — built on every mountain and hill are the covenant desecration of the covenant land's topography. YHWH's covenant judgment dismantles the idolatrous landscape systematically.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 9 — those who escape will remember me among the nations — I have been broken over their whoring heart. YHWH uses the word for being broken — shavar — to describe his own covenant response to Yisra'EL's covenant infidelity. The covenant language is not merely anger but covenant grief: YHWH is broken over the covenant community's departure from him.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 6. The recognition formula — you shall know that I am YHWH — appears multiple times in this chapter and becomes the governing covenant refrain of the entire book of Yehezkel. The purpose of the covenant judgment is covenant recognition: both the covenant community and the nations are brought to covenant knowledge of YHWH through the events of judgment and restoration.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8-9 — yet I will leave some alive — those who escape will remember me and be loathsome in their own sight. The covenant remnant theology: even within the comprehensive judgment, the covenant preservation of a remnant is built in. The covenant purpose of the remnant is covenant memory: they remember YHWH among the nations and are brought to covenant self-judgment for what they have done. The covenant judgment produces covenant repentance in those who survive it.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 7 The End Has Come — The Day of YHWH Upon the Land — Disaster After Disaster — None Shall Remain

1 The word of YHWH came to me:

2 And you, son of man, thus says the Creator YHWH to the land of Yisra'EL: An end. The end has come upon the four corners of the land.

3 Now the end is upon you, and I will send my anger upon you. I will judge you according to your ways, and I will punish you for all your abominations.

4 And my eye will not spare you, nor will I have pity. But I will punish you for your ways, while your abominations are in your midst. Then you will know that I am YHWH.

5 Thus says the Creator YHWH: Disaster after disaster. Behold, it comes.

6 An end has come. The end has come. It has awakened against you. Behold, it comes.

7 Your doom has come to you, O inhabitant of the land. The time has come. The day is near, a day of tumult, and not of joyful shouting on the mountains.

8 Now I will soon pour out my wrath upon you, and spend my anger against you, and judge you according to your ways, and punish you for all your abominations.

9 And my eye will not spare, nor will I have pity. I will punish you according to your ways, while your abominations are in your midst. Then you will know that it is I, YHWH, who strikes.

10 Behold, the day. Behold, it comes. Your doom has come. The rod has blossomed. Pride has budded.

11 Violence has grown up into a rod of wickedness. None of them shall remain, nor their abundance, nor their wealth. Neither shall there be preeminence among them.

12 The time has come. The day has arrived. Let not the buyer rejoice, nor the seller mourn, for wrath is upon all their multitude.

13 For the seller shall not return to what he has sold, while they live. For the vision concerns all their multitude. It shall not turn back. And because of his iniquity, no one can maintain his life.

14 They have blown the trumpet and made everything ready, but none goes to battle, for my wrath is upon all their multitude.

15 The sword is outside, pestilence and famine are inside. He who is in the field dies by the sword, and him who is in the city famine and pestilence devour.

16 And if any survivors escape, they will be on the mountains, like doves of the valleys, all of them moaning, each one over his iniquity.

17 All hands are feeble, and all knees turn to water.

18 They put on sackcloth, and horror covers them. Shame is on all faces, and baldness on all their heads.

19 They cast their silver into the streets, and their gold is like an unclean thing. Their silver and gold are not able to deliver them in the day of the wrath of YHWH. They cannot satisfy their hunger or fill their stomachs with it. For it was the stumbling block of their iniquity.

20 His beautiful ornament they used for pride, and they made their abominable images and their detestable things of it. Therefore I make it an unclean thing to them.

21 And I will give it into the hands of foreigners for prey, and to the wicked of the earth as spoil, and they shall profane it.

22 I will turn my face from them, and they shall profane my treasured place. Robbers shall enter and profane it.

23 Make a chain, for the land is full of bloody crimes and the city is full of violence.

24 I will bring the worst of the nations to take possession of their houses. I will put an end to the pride of the strong, and their holy places shall be profaned.

25 When anguish comes, they will seek peace, but there shall be none.

26 Disaster comes upon disaster. Rumor follows rumor. They seek a vision from the prophet, but the Torah perishes from the priest and counsel from the elders.

27 The king mourns, the prince is wrapped in despair, and the hands of the people of the land are paralyzed by terror. According to their way I will do to them, and according to their judgments I will judge them, and they shall know that I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 7 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 7 is the covenant declaration that the end of the covenant land's present order has arrived. The repetition — the end, the end, it has come, it has awakened — is the covenant rhetoric of irreversibility. This is not a conditional warning but a covenant announcement: the covenant clock has reached its terminus.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 19 — their silver and gold are not able to deliver them in the day of the wrath of YHWH. The covenant economy collapses: the medium of exchange that structures civil life cannot purchase deliverance from covenant judgment. The covenant day of YHWH exposes the covenant emptiness of material security.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 7. Verse 26 — they seek a vision from the prophet, but the Torah perishes from the priest and counsel from the elders — is the covenant communication breakdown: in the day of maximum need, all three channels of covenant guidance — prophetic vision, priestly Torah, elder counsel — fail simultaneously. The covenant community is left without guidance at the moment of covenant catastrophe.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12-13 — the buyer shall not rejoice nor the seller mourn, for wrath is upon all their multitude. The covenant dissolution of normal economic life: the ordinary markers of covenant community life — buying, selling, property exchange — become meaningless. The covenant judgment does not distinguish between economic winners and losers because the entire economic framework is about to be overturned. The covenant day of YHWH levels all human categories.

THE TEMPLE ABOMINATIONS AND THE DEPARTURE OF THE KAVOD — CHAPTERS 8 THROUGH 11

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 8 Yehezkel Transported to Yerushalayim — The Image of Jealousy — The Elders Burning Incense — Women Weeping for Tammuz — Twenty-Five Men With Their Backs to the Temple

1 In the sixth year, in the sixth month, on the fifth day of the month, as I sat in my house, with the elders of Yehudah sitting before me, the hand of the Creator YHWH fell upon me there.

2 Then I looked, and behold, a form that had the appearance of a man. Below what appeared to be his waist was fire, and above his waist was something like the appearance of brightness, like gleaming metal.

3 He put out the form of a hand and took me by a lock of my head, and the Spirit lifted me up between earth and heaven and brought me in visions of YHWH to Yerushalayim, to the entrance of the gateway of the inner court that faces north, where was the seat of the image of jealousy, which provokes to jealousy.

4 And behold, the kavod of the Creator of Yisra'EL was there, like the vision that I had seen in the valley.

5 Then he said to me, Son of man, lift up your eyes now toward the north. So I lifted up my eyes toward the north, and behold, to the north of the altar gate, in the entrance, was this image of jealousy.

6 And he said to me, Son of man, do you see what they are doing, the great abominations that the house of Yisra'EL are committing here, to drive me far from my sanctuary? But you will see still greater abominations.

7 And he brought me to the entrance of the court, and when I looked, behold, there was a hole in the wall.

8 Then he said to me, Son of man, dig in the wall. So I dug in the wall, and behold, there was a door.

9 And he said to me, Go in and see the vile abominations that they are committing here.

10 So I went in and saw. And there, engraved on the wall all around, was every form of creeping things and loathsome beasts, and all the idols of the house of Yisra'EL.

11 And before them stood seventy men of the elders of the house of Yisra'EL, with Ya'azanyahu the son of Shafan standing among them. Each had his censer in his hand, and the smoke of the cloud of incense went up.

12 Then he said to me, Son of man, have you seen what the elders of the house of Yisra'EL are doing in the dark, each in his room of pictures? For they say, YHWH does not see us, YHWH has forsaken the land.

13 He said also to me, You will see still greater abominations that they are committing.

14 Then he brought me to the entrance of the north gate of the house of YHWH, and behold, there sat women weeping for Tammuz.

15 Then he said to me, Have you seen this, O son of man? You will see still greater abominations than these.

16 And he brought me into the inner court of the house of YHWH. And behold, at the entrance of the temple of YHWH, between the porch and the altar, were about twenty-five men, with their backs to the temple of YHWH, and their faces toward the east, worshiping the sun toward the east.

17 Then he said to me, Have you seen this, O son of man? Is it too light a thing for the house of Yehudah to commit the abominations that they commit here, that they should fill the land with violence and provoke me still further to anger? Behold, they put the branch to their nose.

18 Therefore I will act in wrath. My eye will not spare, nor will I have pity. And though they cry in my ears with a loud voice, I will not hear them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 8 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant vision-tour of the temple abominations is systematically structured: YHWH shows Yehezkel four ascending levels of covenant desecration within the temple compound itself. Each abomination is introduced with you will see still greater abominations — verse 6, 13, 15. The covenant sanctuary has become a catalog of covenant violation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 14 — women weeping for Tammuz. The Mesopotamian fertility deity whose annual death and resurrection cycle was mourned by women at the temple gate. The covenant infiltration of Babylonian religious practice into the temple compound is the covenant context of the exile: the exiles sit in Babylon while the religious leadership in Yerushalayim worships Babylonian deities.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 8. Verse 12 — YHWH does not see us, YHWH has forsaken the land. The theological rationale behind the temple abominations: the covenant community's leadership has concluded that YHWH has disengaged from the covenant land, releasing them to worship the gods of the surrounding peoples. The covenant abandonment theology produces covenant abandonment behavior.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16 — twenty-five men with their backs to the temple of YHWH, their faces toward the east, worshiping the sun. The most direct covenant inversion possible: within the temple compound, facing away from the covenant sanctuary toward the east — toward Babylon and the rising sun. The physical posture of turning the back on YHWH's covenant presence to face the sun is the covenant gesture of comprehensive apostasy. The twenty-five elders represent the covenant leadership of Yehudah at its most corrupt.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 9 The Mark on the Foreheads — The Six Men with Weapons — Do Not Spare — Begin at My Sanctuary

1 Then he cried in my ears with a loud voice, saying, Bring near the executioners of the city, each with his destroying weapon in his hand.

2 And behold, six men came from the direction of the upper gate, which faces north, each with his weapon for slaughter in his hand, and with them was a man clothed in linen, with a writing case at his waist. And they went in and stood beside the bronze altar.

3 Now the kavod of the Creator of Yisra'EL had gone up from the cherub on which it rested to the threshold of the house. And he called to the man clothed in linen, who had the writing case at his waist.

4 And YHWH said to him, Pass through the city, through Yerushalayim, and put a mark on the foreheads of the men who sigh and groan over all the abominations that are committed in it.

5 And to the others he said in my hearing, Pass through the city after him, and strike. Your eye shall not spare, and you shall show no pity.

6 Kill old men outright, young men and maidens, little children and women, but touch no one on whom is the mark. And begin at my sanctuary. So they began with the elders who were before the house.

7 Then he said to them, Defile the house, and fill the courts with the slain. Go out. So they went out and struck in the city.

8 And while they were striking, and I was left alone, I fell upon my face, and cried, Ah, Creator YHWH. Are you destroying all the remnant of Yisra'EL in the outpouring of your wrath on Yerushalayim?

9 Then he said to me, The guilt of the house of Yisra'EL and Yehudah is exceedingly great. The land is full of blood, and the city full of injustice. For they say, YHWH has forsaken the land, and YHWH does not see.

10 As for me, my eye will not spare, nor will I have pity. I will bring their deeds upon their heads.

11 And behold, the man clothed in linen, with the writing case at his waist, brought back word, saying, I have done as you commanded me.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 9 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The mark on the foreheads of those who grieve over the abominations is the covenant sign of protection: those who have maintained covenant grief over covenant violation are marked for preservation. The covenant recognition formula is inverted here — those who know YHWH are marked by their grief. The mark distinguishes not the righteous from the wicked by behavior alone but those whose covenant conscience still functions.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 6 — begin at my sanctuary. The covenant judgment starts with the covenant leadership at the covenant center, not with the peripheral covenant community. The most covenant-privileged are the most covenant-accountable. The elders before the house who led the abominations of Chapter 8 are the first to receive the covenant judgment of Chapter 9.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 9. Verse 4 — put a mark on the foreheads of the men who sigh and groan over all the abominations. The covenant remnant in Yerushalayim are identified not by external covenant observance but by internal covenant anguish. The covenant mark is given to those whose hearts are still responsive to covenant violation — those who cannot be indifferent to what is happening in the covenant sanctuary.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — Yehezkel falls on his face and intercedes: Are you destroying all the remnant of Yisra'EL? The covenant prophet's response to the vision of total covenant judgment is covenant intercession. This is the covenant pattern: Mosheh intercedes at the golden calf; Yirmeyahu intercedes repeatedly; now Yehezkel intercedes in the vision itself. YHWH's answer — verse 9-10 — does not reverse the judgment but confirms its covenant basis: the land is full of blood and injustice.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 10 The Coals of Fire — The Cherubim — The Kavod Departs From the Threshold — The Wheels and the Faces

1 Then I looked, and behold, on the expanse that was over the heads of the cherubim there appeared above them something like a sapphire, in appearance like a throne.

2 And he said to the man clothed in linen, Go in among the whirling wheels underneath the cherubim. Fill your hands with burning coals from between the cherubim, and scatter them over the city. And he went in before my eyes.

3 Now the cherubim were standing on the south side of the house, when the man went in, and a cloud filled the inner court.

4 And the kavod of YHWH went up from the cherub to the threshold of the house, and the house was filled with the cloud, and the court was filled with the brightness of the kavod of YHWH.

5 And the sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard as far as the outer court, like the voice of the Almighty when he speaks.

6 And when he commanded the man clothed in linen, Take fire from between the whirling wheels, from between the cherubim, the man went in and stood beside a wheel.

7 And a cherub stretched out his hand from between the cherubim to the fire that was between the cherubim, and took some of it and put it into the hands of the man clothed in linen, who took it and went out.

8 The cherubim appeared to have the form of a human hand under their wings.

9 And I looked, and behold, there were four wheels beside the cherubim, one beside each cherub, and the appearance of the wheels was like sparkling beryl.

10 And as for their appearance, the four had the same likeness, as if a wheel were within a wheel.

11 When they went, they went in any of their four directions without turning as they went, but in whatever direction the front wheel faced the others followed without turning as they went.

12 And their whole body, their rims, their spokes, their wings, and the wheels were full of eyes all around — the wheels that the four of them had.

13 As for the wheels, they were called in my hearing, the whirling wheels.

14 And every one had four faces: the first face was the face of the cherub, and the second face was a human face, and the third the face of a lion, and the fourth the face of an eagle.

15 And the cherubim mounted up. These were the living creatures that I saw by the Kevar river.

16 And when the cherubim went, the wheels went beside them. And when the cherubim lifted up their wings to mount up from the earth, the wheels did not turn from beside them.

17 When they stood still, these stood still, and when they mounted up, these mounted up with them, for the spirit of the living creatures was in them.

18 Then the kavod of YHWH went out from the threshold of the house and stood over the cherubim.

19 And the cherubim lifted up their wings and mounted up from the earth before my eyes as they went out, with the wheels beside them. And they stood at the entrance of the east gate of the house of YHWH, and the kavod of the Creator of Yisra'EL was over them.

20 These were the living creatures that I saw underneath the Creator of Yisra'EL by the Kevar river, and I knew that they were cherubim.

21 Each had four faces, and each four wings, and underneath their wings the likeness of human hands.

22 And as for the likeness of their faces, they were the faces whose appearance I had seen by the Kevar river. Each one of them went straight forward.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 10 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 10 records the covenant departure of YHWH's kavod from the temple threshold. This is the most theologically catastrophic event in the entire Hebrew prophetic corpus: the covenant presence vacating the covenant sanctuary. YHWH does not merely allow the temple to be destroyed — he departs from it before it falls.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 4 — the kavod of YHWH went up from the cherub to the threshold of the house. The covenant departure is staged and deliberate: the kavod moves from the inner sanctuary to the threshold — pausing — before the final departure of Chapter 11. YHWH does not leave abruptly but withdraws in covenant stages, giving the covenant community every opportunity to recognize what is happening.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 10. The identification of the cherubim of Chapter 10 with the living creatures of Chapter 1 — verse 20 — is the covenant continuity of the vision: the same covenant presence that appeared to Yehezkel in Babylon is the same covenant presence that dwelt in the Yerushalayim temple and is now departing from it. YHWH's kavod is mobile — it moves to the exiles in Babylon and withdraws from the corrupt temple.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — fill your hands with burning coals from between the cherubim, and scatter them over the city. The coals of judgment that will destroy Yerushalayim come from the covenant sanctuary itself — from between the cherubim in the holy of holies. The covenant fire that once received the covenant offerings now becomes the covenant fire of judgment. The covenant means of approach to YHWH become the covenant instrument of judgment upon those who have perverted them.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 11 The Twenty-Five Counselors — I Will Be a Little Sanctuary — The Heart of Stone Removed — The Kavod Departs From the City

1 The Spirit lifted me up and brought me to the east gate of the house of YHWH, which faces east. And behold, at the entrance of the gateway there were twenty-five men. And I saw among them Ya'azanyahu the son of Azzur, and Pelatyahu the son of Benayahu, princes of the people.

2 And he said to me, Son of man, these are the men who devise iniquity and who give wicked counsel in this city.

3 They say, The time is not near to build houses. This city is the cauldron, and we are the meat.

4 Therefore prophesy against them, prophesy, O son of man.

5 And the Spirit of YHWH fell upon me, and he said to me, Say, Thus says YHWH: So you think, O house of Yisra'EL. For I know the things that come into your mind.

6 You have multiplied your slain in this city and have filled its streets with the slain.

7 Therefore thus says the Creator YHWH: Your slain whom you have laid in the midst of it, they are the meat, and this city is the cauldron, but you shall be brought out of the midst of it.

8 You have feared the sword, and I will bring the sword upon you, declares the Creator YHWH.

9 And I will bring you out of the midst of it and give you into the hand of foreigners and execute judgments upon you.

10 You shall fall by the sword. I will judge you at the border of Yisra'EL, and you shall know that I am YHWH.

11 This city shall not be your cauldron, nor shall you be the meat in the midst of it. I will judge you at the border of Yisra'EL.

12 And you shall know that I am YHWH. For you have not walked in my statutes, nor obeyed my rules, but have acted according to the rules of the nations that are around you.

13 And it came to pass, while I was prophesying, that Pelatyahu the son of Benayahu died. Then I fell down on my face and cried out with a loud voice and said, Ah, Creator YHWH. Will you make a full end of the remnant of Yisra'EL?

14 And the word of YHWH came to me:

15 Son of man, your brothers, even your brothers, your kinsmen, the whole house of Yisra'EL, all of them, are those of whom the inhabitants of Yerushalayim have said, Go far from YHWH. To us this land is given for a possession.

16 Therefore say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: Though I removed them far off among the nations, and though I scattered them among the countries, yet I have been a little sanctuary to them in the countries where they have gone.

17 Therefore say, Thus says the Creator YHWH: I will gather you from the peoples and assemble you out of the countries where you have been scattered, and I will give you the land of Yisra'EL.

18 And when they come there, they will remove from it all its detestable things and all its abominations.

19 And I will give them one heart, and a new spirit I will put within them. I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh,

20 That they may walk in my statutes and keep my rules and obey them. And they shall be my people, and I will be their Creator.

21 But as for those whose heart goes after their detestable things and their abominations, I will bring their deeds upon their own heads, declares the Creator YHWH.

22 Then the cherubim lifted up their wings, with the wheels beside them, and the kavod of the Creator of Yisra'EL was over them from above.

23 And the kavod of YHWH went up from the midst of the city and stood on the mountain that is on the east side of the city.

24 And the Spirit lifted me up and brought me in the vision by the Spirit of YHWH into Chaldea, to the exiles. Then the vision that I had seen went up from me.

25 And I told the exiles all the things that YHWH had shown me.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 11 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 16 — I have been a little sanctuary to them in the countries where they have gone — is the covenant theological revolution of the exile period: YHWH's covenant presence is not exclusively housed in the Yerushalayim temple. In the countries of exile, YHWH himself is the covenant sanctuary for the scattered covenant community. The kavod visible to Yehezkel in Babylon confirms this covenant claim.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verses 19-20 — I will give them one heart and a new spirit — I will remove the heart of stone and give them a heart of flesh — that they may walk in my statutes. The covenant heart-transformation promise that anticipates the new covenant of Yirmeyahu 31:33. The DSS and Orit traditions preserve these covenant promises as the covenant solution to the covenant problem that produced the exile.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 11. Verse 23 — the kavod of YHWH went up from the midst of the city and stood on the mountain to the east. The final stage of the covenant departure: from the inner sanctuary to the threshold — Chapter 10:4 — from the threshold to the east gate — Chapter 10:19 — and now from the city entirely to the Mount of Olives to the east. The covenant withdrawal is complete.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — Pelatyahu died while Yehezkel was prophesying against him. The covenant sign within the vision: the covenant judgment word accomplishes its purpose in real time. Yehezkel is prophesying in Babylon about leaders in Yerushalayim — and one of them dies at the moment of the vision. The covenant word's power is not limited by distance. Yehezkel immediately intercedes — will you make a full end of the remnant? The covenant prophet does not take satisfaction in the death of the covenant wicked.

END OF PART ONE — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 11

Volume Nineteen — Yehezkel — Part One
